exemplar.title: How do different temperatures (37Â°C, 41Â°C, 42Â°C, 43Â°C, 45Â°C, 47Â°C, and 50Â°C) effect the Homo sapiens breast cancer cell line MDA-MB-231 measured by the number of necrotised cells on a heat plate, heat shock protein concentration, and organelle deterioration?

detailedExemplarData.title: How do different temperatures (37Â°C, 41Â°C, 42Â°C, 43Â°C, 45Â°C, 47Â°C, and 50Â°C) effect the Homo sapiens breast cancer cell line MDA-MB-231 measured by the number of necrotised cells on a heat plate, heat shock protein concentration, and organelle deterioration?

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: The topic is communicated accurately and effectively, with a precise title and clear explanation of the biological context.
The research question is sharply focused, clearly stated, and consistently addressed throughout the report.
Background and rationale subsections provide comprehensive context and a strong justification for the choice of temperature as the independent variable.
weaknesses: The methodological review relies on only two secondary sources and lacks primary data collection.
Inconsistent temperature notation (e.g., incorrect null-hypothesis symbols) and occasional language slips (âtrigerredâ, âdistruptedâ) reduce polish.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall performance in Focus and Method aligns with the top-band descriptors: clear and effective topic communication, a well-focused research question, and coherent rationale. Despite the limited range of methods, the student demonstrates comprehensive explanation and justification. Full marks (6/6) reflect this high-quality execution within the 5â6 band.

strengths: Source material is integrated coherently to support claims about necrosis thresholds, HSP expression, and assay selection.
Key terminology (e.g., hyperthermia, necrosis, heat-shock proteins, clonogenic assay) is used accurately and consistently.
Comparative methodological tables and graphical data interpretations demonstrate effective application of sources.
weaknesses: Several typographical and spelling errors (e.g., âhypethermiaâ, âagressiveâ) distract from precision.
Some citations lack complete details (dates, page numbers), and assertions about ideal temperatures are made without statistical backing.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs application of source material is consistently relevant and demonstrates clear, coherent knowledge. Terminology and concepts are employed accurately. Minor lapses in spelling and referencing are outweighed by strong integration of studies. Full marks (6/6) recognize the workâs overall effectiveness within the 5â6 band.

strengths: Analysis of trends (percentage-difference calculations, lethality curves) is clearly linked to the research question.
The argument follows a logical progression, synthesizing findings from multiple studies.
Conclusions are grounded in data comparisons and suggest concrete methodological improvements.
weaknesses: Reliance on secondary data limits depth; no primary data or statistical testing is conducted.
Evaluation is present but relatively brief and qualitative rather than fully critical.
Some argumentative leaps (e.g., declaring 50 Â°C âidealâ without addressing clinical viability) remain unaddressed.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Critical Thinking performance sits solidly in the high end of the 4â6 band for each strand and approaches the 7â9 band overall. The student delivers good analysis, coherent argument, and supported evaluation. Given the absence of primary data and limited depth in evaluation, a score of 10/12 accurately reflects strong but not exceptional critical thinking.

strengths: The report features a clear, appropriate structure with a detailed table of contents and numbered chapters.
All required sections (introduction, methods, analysis, conclusion, evaluation, bibliography) are present and logically ordered.
weaknesses: Formatting inconsistencies appear in misaligned tables, mixed equation notation, and occasional LaTeX fragments.
Broken hyperlinks and uneven table borders interrupt flow and detract from overall polish.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation is consistently structured and navigable, fulfilling top-band descriptors for organization. However, frequent layout and formatting faults prevent full marks. A score of 3/4 reflects strong structural adherence tempered by those persistent formatting issues.

strandSelections.reasoning: The essay contains a detailed table of contents, numbered chapters and logical sub-headings.  Each required section (introduction, methods, analysis, conclusion, evaluation, bibliography) is present and appropriately ordered, fulfilling the âclear and appropriate structure throughoutâ descriptor; full marks within this 3-4 band are earned.

Page numbers, title, and word count are present, and tables/figures are integrated reasonably.  However, there are formatting inconsistencies: misaligned tables, mixed equation notation, occasional LaTeX code fragments and broken hyperlinks.  These minor but frequent issues indicate that some layout considerations are incorrectly applied, matching a score at the top of the 1-2 band or bottom of 3-4.  A cautious 3 within the 1-2 band ceiling is chosen.

The essay follows a logical progression (introduction â hypothesis â variables â methods â data â conclusion) and maintains a coherent line of reasoning that supports the alternative hypothesis.  Nevertheless, some argumentative jumps (e.g., declaring 50 Â°C âidealâ without addressing clinical viability) and minor inconsistencies prevent the sustained rigour of the 7-9 band.  A solid 4 is therefore appropriate.

The student introduces hyper-thermia treatment clearly, explains its biological basis and links it explicitly to the aggressive, triple-negative MDA-MB-231 line.  Background subsections (1.1â1.4) give coherent, accurate scientific context and the rationale for selecting temperature as the independent variable is explicit.  Overall the topic is communicated accurately and with comprehensive explanation, though occasional language slips (âtrigerredâ, âdistruptedâ) slightly reduce polish, so a strongânot perfectâperformance within the 5â6 band is justified.

Source material from both 2012 and 2014 studies is applied to support claims about necrosis thresholds, HSP expression and spatial heat gradients.  The student extracts numerical values, constructs percentage-difference calculations and interprets graphs.  Nevertheless, much narrative remains descriptive and some extrapolations (e.g., âideal temperature is 50 Â°Câ) are asserted without statistical treatment.  This demonstrates mostly relevant, partially effective use, meriting the upper end of the 3-4 band.

The student analyses trends from three figures, calculates percentage differences and compares time-temperature lethality curves.  The analysis is clearly linked to the research question, but depth is limited by reliance on secondary data and absence of statistical testing.  This constitutes good, but not excellent, analysis â comfortably within the 4-6 band; a mid-high score of 5 is appropriate.

The student relies on two secondary experimental papers and collates their procedures in a comparative table.  While this shows some selection rationale, the range of methods is narrow (only two sources, no primary data collection) and several variables listed (e.g. organelle deterioration) are never methodically measured in the studentâs own work.  Thus methodology is generally relevant but only partially complete, matching the 3-4 band; a mid-band score of 3 is appropriate.

The conclusion synthesises findings from all data sets and acknowledges limitations such as lack of raw data and imaging overlap.  Evaluation of methodology, while present, is brief and largely qualitative.  Suggested improvements (homogeneous heater, advanced imaging) show insight but are not deeply developed.  Hence the strand fits the 4-6 descriptor, closer to its lower end.

The research question is stated verbatim on the title page, is sharply delimited to seven discrete temperatures and three measurable responses, and is wholly appropriate for IB Biology.  Subsequent chapters consistently return to this question.  Minor phrasing issues (âeffectâ not âeffectâ) prevent a full mark, but focus and clarity clearly meet the top-band descriptor.

Key biological termsâhyperthermia, necrosis, heat-shock proteins, clonogenic assayâare used correctly and consistently.  Occasional spelling/grammar lapses and misuse of symbols (e.g., âeffectâ for âaffectâ, temperature formatting errors) prevent the consistency expected for the 5-6 band, so a solid 4 is awarded.

annotations.comment: The studentâs title is precise and clearly communicates the investigationâs focus on hyperthermia treatment variables and the MDA-MB-231 cell line, establishing a strong foundation for the essay.
The table of contents is well organised with each chapter and sub-section clearly listed, enhancing navigability and demonstrating coherent structure.
Page numbers in the footer are present but could be right-aligned for consistency with standard report formatting guidelines.
Some entries in the table of contents table appear misaligned in column formatting. Revising cell width or table margins would improve readability.
There is a typographical error (âtrigerredâ) in the description of mutation triggers. Correct spelling is crucial for precision in scientific writing.
The description of p53âs role in DNA repair and apoptosis is accurate and demonstrates solid understanding of tumour suppressor mechanisms.
This paragraph is very dense. Breaking it into shorter sentences or adding sub-headings would enhance readability and strengthen presentation.
Several source citations (e.g., Sergey Roussakow 2013) lack proper date or page numbers. Complete referencing improves credibility.

In the research question, âeffectâ should be âaffectâ to correctly describe how temperature influences cell behaviour. Precise language ensures the question aligns with IB Biology terminology.
The word count is clearly provided at the start, meeting the Extended Essay formatting requirements and demonstrating attention to administrative detail.
âAgressiveâ is misspelled in this section. Correct spelling (âaggressiveâ) is essential when using technical terminology.
The historical overview of modern hyperthermia research (Crile Jr. experiments) provides valuable context and demonstrates effective use of source material.
Typo detected: âhypethermiaâ should be âhyperthermia.â Consistent spelling is critical for scientific accuracy.
Methodology is limited to two secondary studies. Including a primary data collection or a third paper would strengthen the investigationâs completeness.
The null hypothesis notation â(>\frac{37}{Â°}C)â is incorrect and confusing. Use standard $$^{\circ}\!C$$ notation and clear inequality symbols.
The methods table above the heading is missing procedural details. Complete entries to ensure methodology appropriateness and clarity.
The comparative methods table is helpful but lacks clear step labels for unmatched procedures. Adding identifiers would improve comparative analysis.
The student effectively compares two studiesâ methodologies side by side, demonstrating strong integration of source methods.
The rationale for selecting temperature as the independent variable is well argued, linking back to literature on necrosis thresholds and HSP expression.
Rewrite both hypotheses with consistent temperature notation (e.g., $$37^{\circ}\text{C}$$) and clear directional language to enhance focus and clarity.
In Table 1, heat shock protein concentration is misclassified as a controlled variable when it is a measured response (dependent variable). Reclassify accordingly.
The blank table placeholder on this page interrupts the flow. Ensure all tables are populated with appropriate content or removed if redundant.
Table headings and borders in the cell preparation table are inconsistent. Standardising table style will enhance presentation quality.
Including specific sample sizes and replicates for the clonogenic assay would strengthen methodological rigor and allow for more reliable analysis.
The inclusion of the trypan blue exclusion assay rationale demonstrates clear understanding of cytotoxicity measurement techniques.
The cyclic heat shock method table has empty cells where procedures are unreported. Completing these entries is necessary for a comprehensive methodology.
Provide more detail on how missing cyclic heat shock steps were conducted or justify their omission to improve methodological transparency.
The description of PI staining and incubation times is clear and shows effective application of source methods for assessing necrosis.
